Quentry production environment for Australia region

info.title: Quentry API
info.description:
#### Note: This is a "work in progress" documentatioon. For a released version of the API please refer to: https://developers.quentry.com/RestApi/quentry-v1.0.0/api.html

Before start using the API, you must have Quentry credentials. 
If you haven't done so already, please register at: https://www.quentry.com/ng-WebPortal/#/login/createregistration
Make sure to activate your account using the activation link sent to you at the end of the registration process.

# Basic Usage

The following basic curl commands you can use as a quick start reference. Make sure to replace the MyClientApplicationName in the VoyantClientAppName header with the Application Name provided to you by the Quentry R&D team.
If you don't have application name yet, please contact us at quentry.api@brainlab.com


## 1. Login 
The following examples shows how to make the initial login request in order to obtain session.
Make sure to replace MyPassword and MyUser with your credentials.

Example:
```
    curl --location "https://[ServerUrl]/api/v2/sessions" 
    --header "VoyantClientAppName: MyClientApplicationName" 
    --header "VoyantClientAppVersion: MyClientApplicationVersion" 
    --header "Content-Type: application/json" 
    --data "{
    \"password\": \"MyPassword\",
    \"username\": \"MyUser\"
    }"
```
The result of the login call includes a Session Token and a Web Api endpoint which you __must use__ 
with any additional calls you make to the API. You also get the current user system ID which you 
can use in the next step when uploading files. For Example:

```
{
"data": {
    "dicomWebURL": null,
    "token": "MySessionToken....",
    "region": 2,
    "webAPIURL": "https://[ApiUrl]/api",
    "userSystemId": "12345678-1234-1234-1234-12345678"
  }
}
```

## 2. Upload
Uploading files to Quetry is done in a transctional method in order to ensure the completness of the files
(all or nothing).
### - Start Upload

```
  curl --location "https://[ApiUrl]/api/v2/files" 
  --header "Authorization: Token MySessionToken" 
  --header "VoyantClientAppName: MyClientApplicationName" 
  --header "VoyantClientAppVersion: MyClientApplicationVersion" 
  --header "Content-Type: application/json" 
  --data "{
  \"folderTitle\": \"MyFolderTitle\",
  \"ownerSystemId\":\"RecipientOwnerID\"
  }"
```

As a response for this call you get the unqiue system ID of the folder, an upload id to be used with the next
calls and a unique upload endpoint where you should make the rest of the calls for this specific upload.
For example:

```
{
"data": {
    "folder": {
        "folderSystemId": "1000000000001234567"
    },
    "uploadId": "UploadId",
    "uploadUrl": "https://upload-server.quentry.com:443/api/v2/"
  }
}
```

### - Add File
(Using the uploadId from the previous call)

```
curl --location "https://upload-server.quentry.com:443/api/v2/files/UploadId" 
--header "Authorization: Token MySessionToken" 
--header "VoyantClientAppName: MyClientApplicationName" 
--header "VoyantClientAppVersion: MyClientApplicationVersion" 
--header "Content-Type: multipart/form-data" 
--form "chunkNumber=\"1\"" 
--form "fileName=\"file.dcm\"" 
--form "fileSize=\"111\"" 
--form "isLastChunkInFile=\"true\"" 
--form "uploadId=\"UploadId\"" 
--form "file=@\"/D:/dicoms/801 items/file.dcm\"" 
--form "fileId=\"12345678-1234-1234-1234-12345678\""
```

Response: http 201


### - Finish Upload
(Using the uploadId from the start upload call)
```
curl --location --request PUT "https://upload-server.quentry.com:443/api/v2/files/UploadId" 
--header "Authorization: Token MySessionToken" 
--header "VoyantClientAppName: MyClientApplicationName" 
--header "VoyantClientAppVersion: MyClientApplicationVersion" 
--header "Content-Type: application/json" 
--data "{
\"uploadId\": \"UploadId\"
}"
```

Response: http 200

## 3. Download
The download endpoint let you select the data you want to download by providing the system IDs.
Example:

```
curl --location 'https://api-eu.quentry.com/api/v2/patients/xxx/folders/xxx/download'
--header 'Authorization: Token MySessionToken' 
--header 'VoyantClientAppName: MyClientApplicationName' 
--header 'VoyantClientAppVersion: MyClientApplicationVersion' 
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' 
```

The above call reutrns a response, similar to the following (sensitive data replaced by 'xxx'):

```
{
"data": {
    "zip": "https://production-eu-west-1-download.quentry.com/v1/download/folders/xxx?contextId=xxx&token=xxx",
    "files": [
        {
            "originalFileType": "DICOM",
            "dicomMetadata": {
                "studyInstanceUid": "xxx",
                "seriesUid": "xxx",
                "instanceUid": "xxx",
                "frameNumber": null,
                "ordinalSlicePosition": 0,
                "sopClassUid": "1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.4",
                "modality": "MR"
            },
            "systemMetadata": {
                "studySystemId": "xxx",
                "seriesSystemId": "xxx",
                "instanceSystemId": "xxx"
            },
            "mimeType": "application/dicom",
            "downloadUrl": "xxx",
            "fileSystemId": "xxx"
        },
      ]
    }
  }
```
The response includes download URLs for getting the data as compressed zip file or extracted files.

The download API also provides with direct URLs to spcific study or Series:
```
https://api-eu.quentry.com/api/v2/patients/xxx/folders/xxx/studies/xxx/download
https://api-eu.quentry.com/api/v2/patients/xxx/folders/xxx/studies/xxx/serieses/xxx/download
```

If you don't know the folder, study or series IDs, you can retrieve them from the search endpoint
response which also returns a link to the donwload endpoint. Exaple search response:

```
        ...
        "download-link": "/patients/xxx/folders/xxx/download"
        ...
        "download-link": "/patients/xxx/folders/xxx/studies/xxx/serieses/xxx/download",
        ...
```

# Detailed API
The following lists the supported API calls.

401.description: invalid sesion or session expired. Please login again.

post.description: Perform a login operation to the Quentry platform. A session token is returned upon a successfull login.

401.description: Login failed

Code.description:
User is unauthorized to access the system. The following error code may return in case of unauthorized access.

50102 - Invalid login credentials (wrong user name or password), please try again.

50104 - Incomplete agreements signs (user has not signed the required agreements). Please login to the Web Portal and complete all agreement signing.

50105 - User is blocked, please contact support.

50106 - User is blocked (number of allowed login attempts exceeded). Please reset your password.

50111 - Password has expired and must be changed.

5010113 - User is not active. Please use the first time activation key provided by mail after registration.

50114 - Specific license regulatory documents signing is missing.

50116 - User does not have the required license in order to access Quentry.

Message.example: Invalid login credentials (wrong user name or password), please try again.

delete.description: Close the session and logout from the system

401.description: invalid sesion or session expired. Please login again.

post.description: The client application should call this endpoint with the MFA challenge that returned from the call to the /sessions endpoint togher with the MFA code from the user. A session token is returned upon a successfull login.

401.description: Login failed

Code.description:
User is unauthorized to access the system. The following error code may return in case of unauthorized access.

50102 - Invalid login credentials (wrong user name or password), please try again.

50104 - Incomplete agreements signs (user has not signed the required agreements). Please login to the Web Portal and complete all agreement signing.

50105 - User is blocked, please contact support.

50106 - User is blocked (number of allowed login attempts exceeded). Please reset your password.

50111 - Password has expired and must be changed.

5010113 - User is not active. Please use the first time activation key provided by mail after registration.

50114 - Specific license regulatory documents signing is missing

50116 - User does not have the required license in order to access Quentry.

10081 - Missing MFA challenge data

10082 - MFA wrong code

10083 - MFA code expired

10084 - General error, please contact support.

Message.example: Invalid login credentials (wrong user name or password), please try again.

get.summary: Search for dynamic forms by some search parameters
get.description: Search for forms. Example: api/v2/forms/search?formName=myForm&formVersion=1.0&criterias[0].field=data.b&criterias[0].value=val1&patientSystemId=12345

parameters.name: formName
parameters.description: the name of the form to include in the search

parameters.name: formVersion
parameters.description: the version of the form to include in the search

parameters.name: fieldFullPath
parameters.description: the path of the form field to search

parameters.name: fieldValue
parameters.description: the value to search for

200.description: one or more eForms for this patient

result.description: system IDs of the forms found in the search

401.description: invalid sesion or session expired. Please login again.

403.description: unauthorized. You do not have permissions to delete this form.

get.summary: Retrieve dynamic form by its ID
get.description: Get a form

parameters.name: formSystemId
parameters.description: the unique system ID of the form

200.description: one or more eForms for this patient

401.description: invalid sesion or session expired. Please login again.

403.description: unauthorized. You do not have permissions to delete this form.

parameters.name: formSystemId
parameters.description: the unique system ID of the form

401.description: invalid sesion or session expired. Please login again.

403.description: unauthorized. You do not have permissions to delete this form.

parameters.name: formSystemId
parameters.description: the unique system ID of the form

401.description: invalid sesion or session expired. Please login again.

403.description: unauthorized. You do not have permissions to delete this form.

post.description: Search for patients and its data based on some criterias.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before returning the results

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of items to return

criterias.description: optional fields of search criterias on different levels (patient, folder, dicom data)

patientName.description: optional. filter by patient name DICOM tag [0010,0010]

patientId.description: optional. filter by patient ID DICOM tag [0010,0020]

patientSex.description: optional. filter by patient sex DICOM tag [0010,0040]

patientDateOfBirth.description: optional. filter by patient DOB DICOM tag [0010,0030] (YYYY-MM-DD)

folderModifiedFrom.description: optional. filter by folder modified from specific date (YYYY-MM-DD)

folderModifiedTo.description: optional. filter by folder modified to specific date (YYYY-MM-DD)

folderUploadedFrom.description: optional. filter by uploaded from specific date (YYYY-MM-DD)

folderUploadedTo.description: optional. filter by uploaded to specific date (YYYY-MM-DD)

patientSystemId.description: optional. search for data of a specific patient by its ID

ownerSystemId.description: optional. filter by the system unique identifier of the patient owner (careteam or user)

select.description: specify the requested result set from the search

resultLevel.description:
optional. Define the result return level. The following levels are supported: 
Patient, Folder, Study, Series

 The result includs all level above it. For example, choosing Study will also return Patient and Folder.

 Default value is Patient

criteriasOperator.description: optional. define the logical search operator between the differnt search criterias

200.description: one or more patients for this client

401.description: invalid sesion or session expired. Please login again.

post.description: Use this API for creating a patient in a Quentry account. In Quentry, a patient belongs to an account and may have one of more folders, eForms and other metadata. This API allows you to create the basic patient metadat information. As a result you will get the internal Quentry patient system identifier which can be used with other API calls for adding data for the patient modifying or deleting it. The API returns an existing patient system ID in case it already exists with returned http code of 409.

requestBody.description: the details about the patient to be created.

401.description: invalid sesion or session expired. Please login again.

412.description: Precondition failed. Please check the data that was sent to the server

parameters.description: the unique system ID of the patient

200.description: the details about the requested patient

delete.description: Delete a patient. This will delete the entire patient, and all of its data including all of its folders. There is no way back!

409.description: operation could not be completed due to duplicate patient demographics. The selected update patient details already exists in Quentry. Please use the 'Merge' method in order to merge the two patients together.

patch.description: Merge two patients together. This will merge all patient data includindg, folders, eForm and any demographics with the selected target patietns. This operation triggers a reconciliation on all DICOM files belongs to this patient. The origin patient system ID will no longer be available after this operation.

parameters.name: patientSystemId

parameters.name: otherPatientSystemId

parameters.name: folderSystemId

201.description: operation completed succesfully and a new resource created.

post.description: Start uploading multiple files to a new folder. A new patient will be created in case it does not yet exists, based on the DICOM data that is uploaded.

folderTitle.description: the title of the new folder to be created for this upload

ownerSystemId.description: the owner of the new folder to be created (CareTeam or User)

uploadId.description: the upload identifier to be used for the upload of each file.

uploadUrl.description: a base url for the additional calls of each file to be uploaded.

folder.description: optional. Contais data about the folder that was created for this upload

401.description: invalid sesion or session expired. Please login again.

post.description: Continue upload to a folder. File can be splitted into multiple chunks. Make a call for each chunk specifying the chunk number and the total chunks containing in the file. The server will start processing the file only after all chunks have been arrived. Make sure to use the endpoint URL provided to you in the response to the Start Upload call.

parameters.name: uploadId
parameters.description: the upload identifier that was returned in the Start Upload request

fileSize.description: the total size of the file in bytes

isLastChunkInFile.description: set to true if this is the last chunk of the file or if this is a single file upload flow

chunkNumber.description: the current chunk number of the file. This parameter is required when splitting large files into multiple chunks.

fileId.description: unique GUID identifier of the file. This parmeter is only required when splitting large files into multiple chunks.

401.description: invalid sesion or session expired. Please login again.

put.description: Finish the upload process. Make sure to use the endpoint URL provided to you in the response to the Start Upload call.

parameters.name: uploadId
parameters.description: the upload identifier that was returned in the Start Upload request

401.description: invalid sesion or session expired. Please login again

parameters.name: patientSystemId
parameters.description: the unique system ID of the patient

parameters.name: folderSystemId
parameters.description: the unique system ID of the folder to upload files

uploadId.description: the upload identifier to be used for the upload of each file.

uploadUrl.description: a base url for the additional calls of each file to be uploaded.

401.description: invalid sesion or session expired. Please login again.

post.description:
Continue upload to a folder. 
  
  File can be splitted into multiple chunks. Make a call for each chunk specifying the chunk number and the total chunks containing in the file. The server will start processing the file only after all chunks have been arrived.
            
            
  __Note:__ for transactional upload this call should use the base URL provided to you in the GET response.

parameters.name: patientSystemId
parameters.description: the unique system ID of the patient

parameters.name: folderSystemId
parameters.description: the unique system ID of the folder to upload files

parameters.name: uploadId
parameters.description: the upload identifier that was returned in the Start Upload request

fileSize.description: the total size of the file in bytes

isLastChunkInFile.description: set to true if this is the last chunk of the file or if this is a single file upload flow

chunkNumber.description: the current chunk number of the file. This parameter is required when splitting large files into multiple chunks.

fileId.description: unique GUID identifier of the file. This parmeter is only required when splitting large files into multiple chunks.

401.description: invalid sesion or session expired. Please login again.

parameters.name: patientSystemId
parameters.description: the unique system ID of the patient

parameters.name: folderSystemId
parameters.description: the unique system ID of the folder to upload files

parameters.name: uploadId
parameters.description: the upload identifier that was returned in the Start Upload request

200.description: upload ID completed succesfully. Upload finished.

define the folder detailed level to be returned. The following levels are supported: 
Everything, Study, Series, Instance, Comment, Attachment.
You can choose multiple fields to be returned. "Study, Series" are selected by default in case no value specified.

post.description: Uploads dicom files via DICOM Web. You can either upload to spcific folder (send the PatientSystemId and FolderSystemId headers) or to recipient (send the Recipient and FodlerTitle headers).

file.description: use contentType with the requested transfer syntax, for example: contentType: 'application/dicom;transfer-syntax="1.2.840.10008.1.2.1"'

parameters.name: FolderSystemId
parameters.description: The Folder ID that you want to upload to

parameters.name: PatientSystemId
parameters.description: The Patient ID that you want to upload to

parameters.name: Recipient
parameters.description: The Recipient ID to upload the folder to

parameters.name: FolderTitle
parameters.description: The newly created folder title. can be left empty - random folder title will be generated

401.description: invalid sesion or session expired. Please login again.

412.description: Precondition failed. Please check the data that was sent to the server

get.description: Get current system status and planned downtimes

description.description: Description about the reason for the downtime

Authorization.name: Authorization
Authorization.description: The authentication session token provided to you during login or the JWT for M2M flow supported endpoints (see 'M2M' tag)

VoyantClientAppName.name: VoyantClientAppName
VoyantClientAppName.description: The client application name as it was provided to you by Brainlab.

Version.name: VoyantClientAppVersion
Version.description: The client application version as registered in the Quentry platform.

Compression.name: compression
Compression.description: the compression quality value (only relevant for lossy format types like jpeg)

Width.name: width
Width.description: the requested width of the image. this is only relevant for extracting images out of DICOM files. if both width and height values are provided, the returned image will be adjusted to fit the requested size while keeping the aspect ratio

Height.name: height
Height.description: the requested height of the image. this is only relevant for extracting images out of DICOM files. if both width and height values are provided, the returned image will be adjusted to fit the requested size while keeping the aspect ratio

token.description: the session token to be used with the following API calls

region.description: the geographical region the user belongs to. 1-EU, 2-US, 5-AU

webAPIURL.description: the base API url to be used for making calls on behalf of this user

dicomWebURL.description: the base dicom web api url

MfaRequired.description: Returned in case Multi Factor Authentication is configured for the user

code.description: The MFA code that was sent to the user

folderSystemId.description: the folder system ID where this form is stored

patientSystemId.description: the patient system ID this form belongs to

behavior.description:
defines the logic of submitting a a form:

Add - Create the form anyway (even if an instance of this form already exists) 

Override - If an instance of this form already exists - override it  

IgnoreIfDuplicate - If an instance of this form already exists - a new one will not be created

keywords.description: array of keywords that will be used for searching this form. This is usually based on the values of the form

formDisplayName.description: a human friendly name of the form

exportName.description: optional. form display name when being exported to excel file (usualy from account export or download of a folder).

items.description: the name of the lookup as it is referenced in the form fields

name.description: a user friendly display name of the item

key.description: a key uniquely identifies this item in the lookup

totalFormFields.description: the total number of fields (including mandatory fields) in the form

filledFormFields.description: how many of the fields completed by the user

totalMandatoryFormFields.description: the total number of mandatory fields in the form

filledMandatoryFormFields.description: how many of the mandatory fields completed by the user

formSubmitted.description: indicates whether the form is submitted

state.description: optional. describe the form completenece status. For example,  the form may have 5 fields in total, 3 of them are mandatory. The user completed 1 mandatory and 1 non-mandatory fields.

formData.description: the form data. this data is not known to the server and stored as dynamic json data

example.name_s: these are my favorite days of the week

formSystemId.description: The form unique Quentry system ID

FormInfo.description: describe basic information  of related form

prefix.description:
Parts that come before the name
This repeating element order: Prefixes appear in the correct order for presenting the name
  example: Mr.

suffix.description:
Parts that come after the name
This repeating element order: Suffixes appear in the correct order for presenting the name

HumanName.description: describe the building blocks of patient name. Also not mandatory, at least one of the fields should include some data.

birthdate.description: date of birth of the patient in format YYYY-MM-DD

systemId.description: the internal system identifier of the series

thumbnail-url.description: url for accessing a thumbnail representing this series. The url returns a based64 encoded binary data of the image that can be accessed with a simple http GET request

modality.description: the modality as described in the DICOM tag [0008,0060]

seriesDescription.description: the series description as described in the DICOM tag [0008,103E]

seriesDate.description: the series date as described in the DICOM tag [0008,0021]

seriesTime.description: the series time as described in the DICOM tag [0008,0031]

seriesUid.description: the series UID as described in the DICOM tag [0020,000E]

sopClassUid.description: the SOP class UID as described in the DICOM tag [0008,0016]

contentDate.description: the content date as described in the DICOM tag [0008,0023]

numberOfSlices.description: the number of slices in the series

download-link.description: url for getting series download details

Series.description: Content produded in a DICOM imaging series

systemId.description: the internal system identifier of the study

submitDate.description: the date of which this study was initially submitted to the system. For example, in the Quentry system, this is the upload date (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ)

download-link.description: url for getting study download details

patientName.description: the patient name as described in the DICOM tag [0010,0010]

patientId.description: the patient ID as described in the DICOM tag [0010,0020]

patientDateOfBirth.description: the patient DOB as described in the DICOM tag [0010,0030] (YYYY-MM-DD)

patientSex.description: the patient sex as described in the DICOM tag [0010,0040]

studyDate.description: the study date as described in the DICOM tag [0008,0020]

studyInstanceUid.description: the study instance uid as described in the DICOM tag [0020,000D]

accessionNumber.description: the accession number as described in the DICOM tag [0008,0050]

studyDescription.description: the study description as described in the DICOM tag [0008,1030]

referringPhysician.description: the referring physician name as described in the DICOM tag [0008,0090]

institution.description: the institution name as described in the DICOM tag [0008,0080]

patientComments.description: the patient comments as described in the DICOM tag [0010,4000]

Study.description: Content produced in a DICOM imaging study

systemId.description: the internal system identifier of the folder

processing.description: indicates if the folder is currently being processed on the server. folders which are still being processed may not be complete and the client may want to wait before providing user access to such folders

systemId.description: the system ID of the owner (CareTeam or User)

link.description: url for the resource (CareTeam or User)

systemId.description: the system ID of the patient in this study

studiesCount.description: the number of total studies in this folder

lastModifiedDate.description: the last change date and time of this folder (new/deleted files, shared, etc.) YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

download-link.description: url for getting folder download details

shared.description: indicates whether the folder is shared

folder.description: Logical storage of medical data about patient. Also referened as 'Medical Event', 'Event' or 'Case'

systemId.description: the unique system identifier

ownerType.description: The type of the owner of this patient. This field is not mandatory when sending data to the server (for example, when creating or updating a patient)

systemId.description: the system ID of the owner (Account or User)

link.description: url for the resource (Account or User). This field is not mandatory when sending data to the server (for example, when creating or updating a patient)

ownerType.description: The type of the owner of this patient. This field is not mandatory when sending data to the server (for example, when creating or updating a patient)

systemId.description: the system ID of the owner (Account, User or CareTeam)

link.description: url for the resource (Account or User). This field is not mandatory when sending data to the server (for example, when creating or updating a patient)

systemId.description: the unique system identifier

items.description: links to folders of this patient

eventName.description: the name of the event that will be created upon the submission of this form to a folder. When submitting event via forms, the event is only relevant for folder level forms.

eventCategory.description: the category of the event that will be created upon the submission of this form to a folder. A category can contain multiple events. When submitting event via forms, the event is only relevant for folder level forms.

eventDate.description: the date of the event that will be created upon the submission of this form to a folder. Should be sent in the format “yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ”. When submitting event via forms, the event is only relevant for folder level forms.

zip.description: link for downloading all files in a zip archive. This is only supported for downloading original file format.

originalFileType.description: one of the knwon types supported by the system. This is the original file format as it was uploaded and stored in Quentry

studyInstanceUid.description: the study instance uid as described in the DICOM tag [0020,000D] of this file

seriesUid.description: the series instance uid as described in the DICOM tag [0020,000D] of this file

instanceUid.description: the instance uid as described in the DICOM tag [0008,0018] of this file

frameNumber.description: the frame number in this DICOM instance. This is only returned for DICOM extraction to images in order to allow identifying the exact image

studySystemId.description: the unique study identifier in Quentry

seriesSystemId.description: the unique series identifier in Quentry

instanceSystemId.description: the unique instance identifier in Quentry

mimeType.description: known mime type of the file

downloadUrl.description: url for downloading the file. Make sure to use the 'Authorization' header with your access key when accessing this URL and 'Accept' header with the format to be downloaded, 'image/png' or 'image/jpeg'

systemId.description: the unique identifer of the account

link.description: link for retrieving more details about the account

systemId.description: The uniqie identifier of the careteam

name.description: The registereed name of the careteam

systemId.description: The uniqie identifier of the account

name.description: The registereed name of the account

city.description: The registered city of the account

country.description: The registered country of the account

link.description: link for retrieving more details about the CareTeam

systemId.description: the application unique identifier

careteams.description: list of CareTeams that the application has access to

systemId.description: CareTeam system ID

Permissions.description: Permissions this application has for the CareTeam. Read - the application can read CareTeam data, Write - the application can write data to the CareTeam, Retrieve - the application can download data (files) from the CareTeam

members.description: details about the members of the CareTeam. Note: this only returns for CareTeams the user is member of are belongs to account he is admin of.

systemId.description: the unique identifer of the member

name.description: the name of the careteam member

link.description: link for retrieving more details about the careteam member

systemId.description: The uniqie identifier of the user

firstName.description: The registered first name of the user

lastName.description: The registered last name of the user

username.description: The registered username of the user

institution.description: The registered institution of the user

city.description: The registered city of the user

country.description: The registered country of the user

position.description: The registered position of the user. This value is only returned when sending the request for the current user

email.description: The registered email of the user. This value is only returned when sending the request for the current user

speciality.description: The registered speciality. This value is only returned when sending the request for the current user

contacts.description: The contacts of the user (can be either other User or CareTeam). This value is only returned when sending the request for the current user

systemId.description: the unique identifer of the contact

status.description: Specifies the contact relation status. Whether it is 'Pending' for approval or already 'Approved'

permissions.description: The permissions granted to the contact of this user. 'Upload' - grant permissions to the other user to upload new data to this user

link.description: link for retrieving more details about the contact

careteamMemberships.description: The CareTeam memberships of the user. This value is only returned when sending the request for the current user

systemId.description: the unique identifer of the CareTeam

permissions.description: The permissions of this user in the context of the CareTeam

role.description: role of the user in the CareTeam

link.description: link for retrieving more details about the CareTeam

account.description: The account this user is admin of. This value is only returned when sending the request for the current user

systemId.description: The uniqie identifier of the application

startDate.description: The start date in which the application is allowed for the user (YYYY-MM-DD)

endDate.description: The end date in which the application is allowed for the user (YYYY-MM-DD)

expired.description: Whether or not the license already expired

systemId.description: The unique identifier of the application owner. This may not be the current user in case the application was assigned to the user by a different account

forbidden.description: Forbidden. You may not have permissions to perform this operation.
